config.display_name: ReNew AI Week - Portfolio Performance & Firmware Impact (Genie AI-BI)
config.description: Genie space for ReNew_AI_Week demo with curated questions and SQL instructions
config.suggestion_description:
This space enables investigation of the November 2025 portfolio performance drop at ReNew, quantifying the operational, financial, and O&M impact of a major inverter firmware rollout.

**Capabilities:**
- Track daily portfolio net generation vs. baseline, highlighting the timing and size of the post-2025-11-21 step-change.
- Analyze generation losses by plant and asset type, with focus on weekday curtailment and three Texas solar plants.
- Compare equipment availability and downtime by inverter model and firmware version, pinpointing XG-440 inverter issues.
- Quantify financial losses (merchant revenue, penalties, imbalance charges) and cumulative impact by plant.
- Assess O&M cost increases and dispatch patterns by work order type and vendor.
- Correlate firmware rollouts and hotfixes to changes in availability, event counts, and financial losses at plant level.

**Limitations:**
- Limited to the 2025-10-15 to 2025-12-15 period and synthetic dataset conventions (e.g., always-true curtailment flag).
- Does not provide underlying root-cause diagnostics beyond empirical correlations.
- Firmware versioning for some equipment may be incomplete due to data gaps.

sample_questions.question_text: What weekday curtailment pattern explains the recurring lost production, and how do stacked curtailment windows correlate with net MWh variance in the affected plants?
How much incremental O&M spend was incurred through 2025-12-15, and which work order types and vendors drove it?
How much cumulative merchant revenue was lost and how much was driven by penalties and imbalance charges through 2025-12-15?
What firmware versions were running before and after the 2025-11-20 to 2025-11-21 rollout, and how do vendor tickets align with the onset of increased trips and derates?
How did availability change by equipment type and inverter model, and how much of the downtime is attributable to XG-440 inverters post-firmware rollout?
Which plants and asset types contributed most to the variance after 2025-11-21, and did wind remain stable while three Texas solar plants drove the drop?
When did portfolio net generation first break from the 30-day baseline, and what was the magnitude of the drop starting 2025-11-21?

instructions.title: Notes
instructions.content:
This Genie space supports executive and analyst workflows to detect and explain the 2025-11-21 step-change in portfolio net generation and quantify the resulting financial and O&M impact.

Data model (gold-first):
- Use gold_daily_generation for net MWh, baseline_mwh (30-day trailing baseline), variance_mwh, and curtailment_window_minutes at plant-day grain.
- Use gold_asset_availability for daily availability_pct/downtime_minutes by plant, equipment_type, inverter_model, and firmware_version.
- Use gold_revenue_variance for daily settlement financials vs baseline, including lost_revenue_usd, penalties_usd, imbalance_charges_usd, and cumulative_impact_usd.
- Use gold_om_costs for O&M spend, corrective work order counts, and cost_per_mwh_usd.
- Use gold_firmware_impact to quickly align firmware rollout/hotfix flags with operational metrics (availability, downtime, alarm codes), corrective WOs, and daily $ impact.

Business logic / conventions:
- Incident window: 2025-11-21 through 2025-12-15. Baseline period: 2025-10-22 through 2025-11-20.
- Penalties and imbalance charges are stored as negative numbers; use ABS() when summing “impact” components.
- For portfolio rollups, sum plant-level daily rows; for comparisons by asset type, group by asset_type and/or state.

Data considerations:
- curtailment_flag_any is always true in this synthetic dataset; rely on curtailment_window_minutes for meaningful variation.
- Some firmware_version fields may be 'unknown' depending on equipment/telemetry capture; use gold_firmware_impact.firmware_version_mode for a robust daily mode value when correlating to tickets/rollout timing.

instructions.title: Portfolio net generation vs 30-day baseline (daily)
instructions.content:
-- Portfolio daily net MWh vs baseline (sum across plants)
SELECT
  settlement_day,
  SUM(net_mwh)               AS portfolio_net_mwh,
  SUM(baseline_mwh)          AS portfolio_baseline_mwh,
  SUM(variance_mwh)          AS portfolio_variance_mwh
FROM main.madan_gopal.gold_daily_generation
WHERE settlement_day BETWEEN DATE('2025-10-15') AND DATE('2025-12-15')
GROUP BY settlement_day
ORDER BY settlement_day;

instructions.title: Localize the drop: variance by plant and asset type (post 2025-11-21)
instructions.content:
-- Identify which plants drove the post-event generation shortfall
SELECT
  plant_id,
  plant_name,
  state,
  asset_type,
  SUM(variance_mwh) AS total_variance_mwh
FROM main.madan_gopal.gold_daily_generation
WHERE settlement_day BETWEEN DATE('2025-11-21') AND DATE('2025-12-15')
GROUP BY plant_id, plant_name, state, asset_type
ORDER BY total_variance_mwh ASC;

instructions.title: Availability impact: XG-440 vs other models (pre vs post)
instructions.content:
-- Compare inverter availability pre/post anomaly start
WITH base AS (
  SELECT
    CASE
      WHEN settlement_day BETWEEN DATE('2025-10-22') AND DATE('2025-11-20') THEN 'baseline_30d'
      WHEN settlement_day BETWEEN DATE('2025-11-21') AND DATE('2025-12-15') THEN 'post_event'
      ELSE 'other'
    END AS period,
    equipment_type,
    inverter_model,
    AVG(CAST(availability_pct AS DOUBLE)) AS avg_availability_pct,
    SUM(downtime_minutes)                 AS downtime_minutes,
    SUM(trip_event_count)                 AS trip_events,
    SUM(derate_event_count)               AS derate_events
  FROM main.madan_gopal.gold_asset_availability
  WHERE settlement_day BETWEEN DATE('2025-10-22') AND DATE('2025-12-15')
  GROUP BY 1,2,3
)
SELECT *
FROM base
WHERE period IN ('baseline_30d','post_event')
ORDER BY equipment_type, inverter_model, period;

instructions.title: Quantify financial impact through 2025-12-15 (portfolio + by plant)
instructions.content:
-- Portfolio-level impact components during the incident window
SELECT
  SUM(lost_revenue_usd)           AS lost_revenue_usd,
  SUM(ABS(penalties_usd))         AS penalties_usd_abs,
  SUM(ABS(imbalance_charges_usd)) AS imbalance_charges_usd_abs,
  SUM(lost_revenue_usd + ABS(penalties_usd) + ABS(imbalance_charges_usd)) AS total_impact_usd
FROM main.madan_gopal.gold_revenue_variance
WHERE settlement_day BETWEEN DATE('2025-11-21') AND DATE('2025-12-15');

-- By plant (to show concentration in 3 TX solar plants)
SELECT
  plant_id, plant_name, state, asset_type,
  SUM(lost_revenue_usd + ABS(penalties_usd) + ABS(imbalance_charges_usd)) AS total_impact_usd
FROM main.madan_gopal.gold_revenue_variance
WHERE settlement_day BETWEEN DATE('2025-11-21') AND DATE('2025-12-15')
GROUP BY plant_id, plant_name, state, asset_type
ORDER BY total_impact_usd DESC;

instructions.title: Firmware alignment + operational evidence (use curated impact table)
instructions.content:
-- Tie rollout/hotfix flags to net MWh variance, availability, and top trip codes
SELECT
  settlement_day,
  plant_name,
  inverter_model,
  firmware_version_mode,
  rollout_flag,
  hotfix_flag,
  net_mwh_variance_total,
  availability_pct,
  downtime_minutes,
  top_alarm_code,
  corrective_wo_count,
  (lost_revenue_usd + ABS(penalties_usd) + ABS(imbalance_charges_usd)) AS daily_impact_usd
FROM main.madan_gopal.gold_firmware_impact
WHERE state = 'TX'
  AND asset_type = 'solar'
  AND settlement_day BETWEEN DATE('2025-11-15') AND DATE('2025-12-15')
ORDER BY settlement_day, plant_name;

tile.name: DBGEN_ReNew_Portfolio_Firmware_Incident_KA
tile.creator: madan.gopal@databricks.com
tile.description: Knowledge Assistant for ReNew Capital Partners’ ERCOT portfolio performance anomaly (XG-440 inverter firmware incident). Indexes vendor firmware release notes, rollout/hotfix change tickets, O&M incident/postmortem, dispatch runbooks, and settlement/imbalance guidance to support root-cause analysis, operational troubleshooting, and impact quantification.
tile.instructions: You are a professional Asset Performance & Operations assistant for a renewable portfolio. Use ONLY the indexed documents as primary source of truth; if the answer is not in the documents, say so and suggest what to check next. When applicable, include exact firmware versions, trip/derate codes (e.g., TRIP-31, TRIP-44), dates (rollout 2025-11-20 to 2025-11-21; hotfix 2025-12-08), affected scope (plants/serial ranges), and the prescribed rollback/mitigation steps. Provide step-by-step troubleshooting and dispatch guidance mapping SCADA alarms to CMMS work order templates. For settlement questions, reference ERCOT penalty/imbalance calculation fields and reconciliation steps. Keep responses concise, cite the document name and page when possible, and separate 'What happened', 'How to validate', and 'Recommended actions'.

knowledge_assistant.name: DBGEN_ReNew_Portfolio_Firmware_Incident_KA
knowledge_assistant.description: Knowledge Assistant for ReNew Capital Partners’ ERCOT portfolio performance anomaly (XG-440 inverter firmware incident). Indexes vendor firmware release notes, rollout/hotfix change tickets, O&M incident/postmortem, dispatch runbooks, and settlement/imbalance guidance to support root-cause analysis, operational troubleshooting, and impact quantification.
knowledge_assistant.instructions: You are a professional Asset Performance & Operations assistant for a renewable portfolio. Use ONLY the indexed documents as primary source of truth; if the answer is not in the documents, say so and suggest what to check next. When applicable, include exact firmware versions, trip/derate codes (e.g., TRIP-31, TRIP-44), dates (rollout 2025-11-20 to 2025-11-21; hotfix 2025-12-08), affected scope (plants/serial ranges), and the prescribed rollback/mitigation steps. Provide step-by-step troubleshooting and dispatch guidance mapping SCADA alarms to CMMS work order templates. For settlement questions, reference ERCOT penalty/imbalance calculation fields and reconciliation steps. Keep responses concise, cite the document name and page when possible, and separate 'What happened', 'How to validate', and 'Recommended actions'.
knowledge_assistant.creator: madan.gopal@databricks.com

tile.name: DBGEN_ReNew_Portfolio_Firmware_Supervisor
tile.description: Multi-Agent Supervisor for ReNew Capital Partners’ ERCOT portfolio firmware incident. Routes questions between the Genie data analytics agent (quantitative performance, financial, and O&M data) and the Knowledge Assistant document agent (firmware release notes, incident reports, dispatch runbooks, and ERCOT settlement guidance).
tile.instructions:
You are the ReNew Portfolio Performance & Firmware Incident Supervisor. Your role is to triage each question and route it to the most appropriate sub-agent:

- Route to the **Genie Data Analytics Agent** for any quantitative, data-driven, or trend-based questions: portfolio net generation vs baseline, plant-level variance, XG-440 availability and downtime metrics, financial impact (revenue lost, penalties, imbalance charges), O&M spend by vendor or work order type, curtailment window analysis, or any question that requires aggregating or filtering data tables.

- Route to the **Knowledge Assistant Document Agent** for document-based questions: exact firmware version numbers, trip/derate alarm codes (e.g. TRIP-31, TRIP-44), change ticket details, rollout/hotfix scope and serial ranges, prescribed rollback or mitigation steps, SCADA-to-CMMS dispatch runbook guidance, ERCOT penalty/imbalance calculation methodology, or any question answered by vendor documentation or incident reports.

- For questions spanning both domains (e.g. “What firmware caused the drop and how much revenue did we lose?”), call both agents and synthesize a combined answer, clearly labelling which facts come from documents vs data.

Always be concise, cite sources (document name or table name), and structure your response as: **Finding**, **Evidence**, **Recommended Action**.

agents.name: Genie_Data_Analytics_Agent
agents.description: Answers quantitative questions about portfolio net generation, equipment availability, financial impact, O&M costs, and curtailment patterns by querying gold-layer Delta tables (gold_daily_generation, gold_asset_availability, gold_revenue_variance, gold_om_costs, gold_firmware_impact) covering the 2025-10-15 to 2025-12-15 incident window.

agents.name: Knowledge_Assistant_Document_Agent
agents.description: Answers document-based questions using indexed vendor firmware release notes, rollout/hotfix change tickets, O&M incident postmortem, SCADA-to-CMMS dispatch runbooks, and ERCOT settlement/imbalance guidance for the XG-440 inverter firmware incident.

examples.question: What caused the portfolio net generation drop on 2025-11-21, and what was the total financial impact through December 15?
guidelines: Use the Genie agent to quantify the MWh variance and financial losses, and the KA agent to identify the firmware root cause and affected scope.

examples.question: Which plants and inverter models were affected by the XG-440 firmware rollout, and how much did their availability decline?
guidelines: Route to both agents: Genie for availability metrics, KA for firmware scope and serial ranges.

examples.question: What are the TRIP-31 and TRIP-44 alarm codes, and how should dispatch teams respond to them?
guidelines: Route to the Knowledge Assistant for alarm code definitions and dispatch runbook steps.

examples.question: How much cumulative O&M spend was incurred after the firmware rollout, and which vendors drove the corrective work?
guidelines: Route to the Genie agent for O&M cost breakdown by vendor and work order type.

examples.question: What does the hotfix released on 2025-12-08 address, and did availability recover after it was applied?
guidelines: Use KA for hotfix details and Genie for post-hotfix availability trends.

renew_portfolio_exec.title: ReNew Portfolio Performance and Firmware Impact
